title: Shinji Misato A Metaphoric Relationship Ep 2325 Theories
display_title: Shinji Misato A Metaphoric Relationship Ep 2325 Theories
wikitext:
[]: Shinji/Misato: A Metaphoric Relationship [Ep 23,25 Theories] Post by ran1 » Thu Apr 22, 2010 2:30 pm This is the discussion forum. Long posts are par for the course. No warnings are required. - OMF Alright, some serious half-developed stuff here after analyzing a few things for the Shinji and Misato Relationship Article for the wiki. Most of my material for this topic is coming from these episodes: -Episodes 2, 23', 24, 25, 26, 25', 26' Alright, here we go: For the Wiki article , I wrote these following things: I, in the Episode 2 Analysis wrote: The scene in which Misato makes her case to be Shinji's guardian holds particular significance to the Episode 23 Big Irony Bomb scenario. In this scene Misato states the following in her conversation with Ritsuko: (see montage box) Regarding the outset of the situation, Shin-Seiki describes it best: "There's a lot of Freud referenced in NGE, and one of his ideas is that 'unacceptable' thoughts and desires cannot be suppressed entirely: They tend to come to the surface one way or another, "Freudian slips" being one example, or, as we see here, they get expressed as 'mere jokes'; and I would say this is a pretty self-implicating joke for Misato to make. As Ritsuko quite rightly puts it: "What the hell are you thinking!" Why would it even occur to Misato to say such a thing, unless the idea was lurking somewhere, perhaps subconsciously, in the back of her mind?… "

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 22, 2010 3:13 pm Shinji doesn't seem to accept Misato's request* considering how shocked he looks after the kiss as Misato says her goodbyes and pushes him into the elevator. (A moment before the kiss happened, Shinji was agreeing with Misato that he'd promise to find answers of why he's piloting for himself, etc etc etc). I wonder if the script(s) go into what's going on behind those scenes at all... Either in EoTV or EoE, seeing as the infamous 23' scene is here . Speaking of Shinji and piloting, I only recently realized Misato's lines in Episode 01 are echoed in Episode 25': Episode 01 Literal Translation Project wrote: Misato: Shinji-kun... Why did you come here? Don't run away, not from your father and, above all, not from yourself.

[]: Post by ran1 » Thu Apr 22, 2010 3:29 pm SSD wrote: Shinji doesn't seem to accept Misato's request* considering how shocked he looks after the kiss as Misato says her goodbyes and pushes him into the elevator. (A moment before the kiss happened, Shinji was agreeing with Misato that he'd promise to find answers of why he's piloting for himself, etc etc etc).

[]: Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 22, 2010 3:36 pm ran1 wrote: I interpreted that more as Shinji being disgusted with the thought of his guardian/love interest sleeping with his mentor, but I suppose it could be just general dissatisfaction with the act itself.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 22, 2010 3:54 pm Reichu: Well, the chair didn't look phallic in On Air 23, and the Newtype Filmbook saying the scene's meant to be sexual helps affirm the Misato's actions (and of course, Misato in Episode 25)... I do see your point about baiting, but that wasn't my intent. >_>; By the way, Ran, I added some speculation/thoughts of the "What if" scenario here.

[]: Post by ran1 » Thu Apr 22, 2010 4:08 pm Considering Shinji is somewhat of a prude yet also sexually repressed (eg: Asuka and her flirting, seeing Rei naked, Misato's teasing/flirting), things would have been very very awkward if not somewhat angsty as well. ...That is, if Shinji would have actually have had sex with Misato. At the least, I could see Misato forcing the "adult kiss" on him then Shinji pushing her away, literally and emotionally. I think Shinji would have felt ashamed and pissed off at himself (even more so than fapping over Asuka in 25'??) and possibly have been withdrawn from the world by the time 24' started instead of falling into such a withdrawn state at 25'. What this would have changed for Kaworu and Shinji interaction (Would Shinji feel so bitter or withdrawn that he wouldn't even want to fight Kaworu? Would he not hesitate as much with killing him? Hell, would Kaworu manage to cause 3rd Impact and/or would Rei be forced to stop Kaworu herself?) as well the events of EoE, who knows. It's all speculation

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 22, 2010 5:05 pm To clarify, I think it was a mixture of surprise and disgust. Emotions--especially in Eva--are never quite so clear-cut. I suppose the when and where of Misato actually seducing Shinji happening would have a lot to do with his reaction, her reaction, how things would play out from there... More speculation then I'm done: If Shinji and Misato did actually start to have a sexual relationship somewhere around Episode 15 and 16, my guess is Kaji would be disgusted, call the whole thing off between him and Misato before anything really began and Asuka would have more of a reason to hate Misato's personality than she already did. This thread now makes me want to read a "good" M/S fic or doujin.

[]: Post by Grimmjow » Thu Apr 22, 2010 7:00 pm I never considered Shinji/Misato sexual relationship part of fanwankery. After all we know that Shinji desired it to a certain degree since Misato was in his sexual fantasies during episode 20. Misato tries to get close to men through sex because that is the way she deals with her own lonliness. She knows that if she has sex with them, at least she won't be alone. She remembers first hand her many years of just sitting in a room, utterly isolated from everything. We already know from episode 25' that she believes she can heal Shinji's emotional wounds just by screwing him. Thus it is merely a small leap of logic to say that she attempted to do this in episode 23.

[]: Re: Shnji/Misato: A Metaphoric Relationship [Ep 23, 25 Theor Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Apr 22, 2010 7:24 pm These are more points for an editorial thread, but considering this is a Theory and Analysis page, I suppose we may as well have this discussion here (While we're at it, if anyone wants to suggest serious objections to the theory of Misato and Shinji for a mention in the Wiki, now would be a good time) ran1 wrote: So my first question is, regarding the material included in the Wiki article: Am I going too far in saying these things?

[]: Post by ran1 » Thu Apr 22, 2010 7:38 pm OMF wrote: This was the part where I felt you wandered off-topic onto shakier ground.

[]: Re: Shnji/Misato: A Metaphoric Relationship [Ep 23, 25 Theor Post by SenorSquiid » Thu Apr 22, 2010 11:35 pm ran1 wrote: Though I'm still developing this explaination, could Shinji's acceptance of Misato be analgous to an acceptance of love that drives the narrative forward? In structuralist terms, we could see Episode 23 as Shinji's "denial of the [Misato's/Hero's call to action] whereas Episode 25' is Shinji's acceptance of Misato's call to action.

[]: Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 23, 2010 12:45 am ran1 wrote: And, judging by his reaction, a failure as a love interest as well...?

[]: Post by tomrule123 » Fri Apr 23, 2010 10:32 am EXCELLENT WORK, RAN1! Now, I feel bad for Misato. She wants to make those feel better, but somehow make it worse. Shinji never really experiences love that much, which is what Misato was trying to do for Shinji: showing love in the deepest way possible (but to a 14 year old? THAT'S INSANE! But hey, what else could she do?).

[]: Post by TheLobe » Fri Apr 23, 2010 10:59 pm I always thought Misato was just confused. she actually says she doesn't know how to be around him. She's at around the age people often start thinking about children, so she pretty much picks one up. But she's never been in a non-sexual relationship with a man.
